stories.story: As a patient, I want to log in to a patient dashboard so that my booking workflow is separate from staff review.
acceptance_criteria: The patient demo account can log in successfully.
The patient cannot access staff review actions.

stories.story: As a patient, I want to submit an appointment request so that clinic staff can review it.
acceptance_criteria: The request requires name, date, time, and appointment type.
The initial status is Pending Review.
The request appears in the patient's request list.

stories.story: As a doctor, I want to review appointment requests so that I can confirm or cancel bookings safely.
acceptance_criteria: The doctor demo account is redirected to /staff.
The doctor can see the shared appointment queue.
The doctor can confirm, cancel, or reopen a request.

stories.story: As admin staff, I want conflict flags so that I can identify duplicate time slots before confirming appointments.
acceptance_criteria: Two active requests for the same date and time create a conflict flag.
Cancelled requests do not block the slot.
The conflict flag is visible in the staff dashboard.

stories.story: As the clinic system, I want to produce non-diagnostic summaries so that staff can review requests without medical advice generation.
acceptance_criteria: The summary includes administrative booking details.
The summary states that it is non-diagnostic.
The summary does not provide treatment advice.

stories.story: As a developer, I want optional API hooks to be configured through environment variables so that secrets are not committed.
acceptance_criteria: The application works without optional API keys.
